violations: components/SegmentLedger.tsx:122 — business logic in component: `Math.round(r.pd_bps * (1 + i * 0.05))` synthesizes forecast quarter PDs from a base via a linear ramp. The forecast curve is a quantitative-model decision and should be produced by the cecl-pd-lgd-projector net-new atomic service and passed to the component as data (ui-standards Rule 2 + product-build-discipline Rule 8).

Rule 13 (live > polled > static) — no SSE / live event stream surfaced; the rail and ledger are render-time snapshots. The option declares this as a deliberate trade-off in tradeoffs.md (executive review view) but it still scores below an option that surfaces the event spine somewhere.
app/page.tsx:37 — `getRun("")` is called with an empty string to fetch a 'live' run; if the fixture lookup behavior changes the home page can break. Mildly fragile defensive UI (Rule 14).
strengths: Process-as-rail metaphor is the strongest in the run: a 4-stage WorkflowStageRail is visible on home and run-detail with annotations for owner / caption / status.
CFO irrevocable attestation correctly routes through the shared ApprovalGate primitive (no DIY confirm dialog).
Heavy reuse — 9 shared primitives anchor the chrome (AppShell, BreadcrumbNav, WorkflowStageRail, RegulatoryClock, MetricStrip, StatCard, StatusBadge, StepProgress, ApprovalGate).
Server/Client boundary is clean: Server pages hand the Client `RunStageRail` and `StageDrill` only serializable data; no inline functions cross the boundary (UI Rule 4).
Sparse executive density discipline is intentional and persona-justified — three numbers and an OCC RegulatoryClock above the fold.

violations: components/SegmentRow.tsx:74 — Tailwind arbitrary value `w-[3px]` for the risk-band ring gutter. Minor — should resolve to a token (e.g. `border-l-2` or a defined width scale). ui-standards Rule 4.
Rule 13 (live > polled > static) — no SSE on the segment-row surface; the AuditLedger renders a static event stream from the run record at request time. The event spine is present but not live.
strengths: Affordance discipline matches the canvas hitl_gates contract exactly: reversible `approve_segment_methodology` lives inline on the row that surfaces its evidence; irrevocable `cfo_attest_run` is the single screen that escapes inline, routed through the shared ApprovalGate.
AuditLedger is the canonical event-spine surface (Pub/Sub events rendered chronologically with actor type + headline + decision badge) — strongest 'audit-as-SOP' artifact across both options.

Inline reasoning expander on each SegmentRow surfaces the three agent hops (financial-spreader → industry-risk-scorer → segment-reserve-agent) without leaving the row — 'agent activity visible live' principle is concrete.
MethodologyOwnerRail makes the human-in-the-loop axis explicit: each owner with their pending count, in banker vocabulary.
No business logic in components — PD/LGD/EAD/ECL come from the data adapter; SegmentRow only renders.
Defensive UI: the /approval page short-circuits with a 'Gate not in scope' fallback if the canvas drift removes either gate (Rule 14 explicitly satisfied).

recommendation_rationale: Option C scores higher on both axes. On ui_standards: zero business logic in components, defensive UI is explicit (gate-not-in-scope guard), and the only deviation is a single Tailwind arbitrary value (w-[3px]). On agentic_principles: it provides the strongest event-spine surface (AuditLedger renders the Pub/Sub log with actor + decision), the strongest agent-activity-visible-live pattern (per-row reasoning expander naming the three agent hops), and most importantly its affordance topology matches the canvas hitl_gates contract precisely — reversible methodology approvals live inline on the segment row, the single irrevocable CFO attestation escapes to /approval through the shared ApprovalGate. Option A's process-as-rail metaphor is stronger and its executive density discipline is admirable, but its SegmentLedger contains a quantitative-model arithmetic decision (PD forecast curve via `Math.round(r.pd_bps * (1 + i * 0.05))`) — exactly the kind of business logic that must live in the cecl-pd-lgd-projector atomic service, not in a presentation component. Composite: C = 4.3*0.4 + 4.3*0.4 + 1.0 + 0.2 - 0.2 = 4.44; A = 4.2*0.4 + 4.0*0.4 + 1.0 + 0.2 - 0.3 = 4.18.
notes_for_human: ONLY TWO OPTIONS scored. Options B and D were lost when their worktrees were auto-cleaned before the judge pass; the comparator should display a banner explaining the 2-of-4 scoring. The user is invited to re-run the full 4-designer pass if diversity signal is needed.
Both surviving options correctly route CFO irrevocable attestation through the shared ApprovalGate primitive — Rule 1 of product-build-discipline satisfied for both.

Diversity signal is good — Jaccard similarity of components_used is 0.33 (7 shared primitives / 21 union components); the two options express the canvas differently (process-rail vs row-as-disposition).

Option A's PD forecast arithmetic in SegmentLedger.tsx is the single most actionable defect. Even if A is chosen, that computation must move to the cecl-pd-lgd-projector atomic service before promotion.

Neither option surfaces a live SSE event stream — Rule 13 (live > polled > static). Both teams justified this as a deliberate trade-off for a quarterly-cadence run, but the comparator should flag it. If the use case ever needs in-flight monitoring (e.g. CFO watching the rerun during exception review), an SSE backbone will need to be added.
Option C's w-[3px] Tailwind arbitrary value is a token-discipline drift the auditor should flag; trivial to fix (move to a defined width or use border-l-2).
The composite gap (4.44 vs 4.18 = 0.26) is just above the 0.2 tie-break threshold; the human should still verify subjective fit-for-CFO-persona before promoting C.
